title: three.ws on-chain avatar manifest
description: Canonical, hash-anchored description of an on-chain avatar. A signed instance of this document is what three.ws agents resolve when they fetch an avatar by id from any chain.

schemaVersion.description: Version of this manifest schema. Always 1 for documents validated against avatar.v1.json.

id.description: Canonical avatar identifier. Either a CAIP-10 account id (e.g. eip155:1:0xabc...) or a name from a supported name service (.eth, .ws, .sol).

mesh.description: Pointer to the 3D mesh that renders this avatar.

uri.description: Content-addressed or HTTP(S) URI. Prefer ipfs:// or ar:// for immutability.

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the mesh bytes, lowercase hex.

skeleton.description: Skeleton layout the mesh conforms to. Determines which animation libraries can drive it without runtime retargeting.

animations.description: Optional pointer to an animation manifest (list of clips by name → uri/sha256). If omitted, the avatar relies on whatever default clips the host viewer ships.

accessories.description: Equipped accessory items (hats, glasses, etc.). Each entry binds a slot to a content-addressed asset.

traits.description: Free-form key/value traits (NFT-style). String/number/boolean values only; no nested objects.

owner.description: Current on-chain owner. Wallet address scoped by chain id.

address.description: Wallet address. Format depends on chain.

creator.description: Original creator. Omit if same as owner.

signature.description: Optional signature over the canonical JSON serialization of this manifest, with the signature field itself removed. Algorithm-specific.

signer.description: Wallet address that produced the signature. Should match owner.address unless delegated.
